name: Noáin - Sangüesa - Olleta – circular route through the Montes de Valdorba
name_original: Noáin - Sangüesa - Olleta – circular por los Montes de Valdorba
region_intro: Road cycling around Noáin (Valle De Elorz) in Navarra, Spain, features diverse landscapes. The region is characterized by the Elorz River valley, offering flatter sections, and is framed by the Sierra de Alaiz to the south and the Sierra de Tajonar to the north, which provide varied terrain with significant climbs. Agricultural plains and diverse forest types, including beech and oak groves, contribute to the scenic backdrops for road cycling routes. Best road cycling routes around Noáin (Valle De Elorz) The most popular road cycling route is Noáin - Sangüesa - Olleta – circular route through the Montes de Valdorba, a 64.2 miles (103.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 58 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers a circular journey through the Montes de Valdorba. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Citadel of Pamplona – Rabia Bridge, Zubiri loop from Cocheras Noain, a difficult 55.8 miles (89.8 km) path. This route forms a loop passing the Citadel of Pamplona and Rabia Bridge. Local road cyclists also love the View of Goñi Valley – Alto de Guembe (920 m) loop from Cocheras Noain, a 64.2 miles (103.3 km) trail leading through Goñi Valley, often completed in about 5 hours 0 minutes. Road cycling around Noáin (Valle De Elorz) is defined by mountain ranges like Sierra de Alaiz and Sierra de Tajonar, river valleys, and agricultural plains. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Noáin (Valle De Elorz) are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 570 reviews. More than 20,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Noáin (Valle De Elorz)'s varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Noáin (Valle De Elorz)?
A: Noáin (Valle De Elorz) offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 220 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 17 easy, 109 moderate, and 96 difficult trails.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Noáin (Valle De Elorz)?
A: The region's diverse landscapes, from river valleys to mountain ranges, make it suitable for road cycling across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, while summer can be ideal for higher elevation routes. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: What kind of landscapes will I encounter while road cycling in Noáin (Valle De Elorz)?
A: You'll experience a rich variety of landscapes. Routes often traverse the gentle Elorz River valley and expansive agricultural plains, offering picturesque, flatter sections. For more challenging rides, you'll ascend into the Sierra de Alaiz and Sierra de Tajonar mountain ranges, featuring diverse forest types from beech to oak groves, and rewarding panoramic views.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Noáin (Valle De Elorz)?
A: Yes, there are 17 easy road cycling routes in Noáin (Valle De Elorz) that are perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes typically feature less elevation gain and cover gentler terrain, often following river valleys or agricultural plains.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes in the region?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Noáin (Valle De Elorz) boasts 96 difficult road cycling routes. These often involve significant ascents into the Sierra de Alaiz and Sierra de Tajonar. A notable challenging route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40379179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Goñi Valley – Alto de Guembe (920 m) loop from Cocheras Noain&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 100 km with substantial elevation gain.

Q: What landmarks or points of interest can I see along road cycling routes in Noáin (Valle De Elorz)?
A: Many routes offer views of historical and natural landmarks. You might pass by the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/654847" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilgrim Monument at Alto del Perdón&lt;/a&gt;, offering sweeping views, or cycle near the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4817230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magdalena Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. The proximity to Pamplona also means you can easily visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4396525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pamplona Cathedral and the Citadel&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there scenic viewpoints accessible by road bike?
A: Absolutely. The mountain ranges like Sierra de Alaiz and Sierra de Tajonar provide numerous opportunities for scenic viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3690724" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Alto del Perdón&lt;/a&gt; is a particularly popular spot, offering expansive vistas of the surrounding landscapes. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32022465" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the fields – View from Alto del Perdón loop from Cocheras Noain&lt;/a&gt; are designed to incorporate such scenic spots.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with cultural excursions in the area?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history and culture. The historic Camino de Santiago pilgrimage route traverses the area, and you can find medieval bridges and an 18th-century aqueduct. The nearby city of Pamplona, with its famous Citadel and Cathedral, is also easily accessible, allowing you to enrich your cycling tour with cultural exploration.

Q: Is there parking available for cyclists near the road cycling routes?
A: While specific parking areas for cyclists are not explicitly listed, Noáin (Valle De Elorz) is a municipality with various starting points for routes. It is generally advisable to look for public parking facilities in the towns or villages where you plan to begin your ride, such as Cocheras Noain, a common starting point for many routes.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Noáin (Valle De Elorz)?
A: The road cycling routes in Noáin (Valle De Elorz) are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 570 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from challenging mountain climbs to scenic river valleys, and the well-maintained paths that allow for an enjoyable and varied riding experience.

Q: Are there any notable circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in Noáin (Valle De Elorz) are designed as circular loops, offering a convenient way to explore the region. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986477047/noain-sangueesa-olleta-circular-route-through-the-montes-de-valdorba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Noáin - Sangüesa - Olleta – circular route through the Montes de Valdorba&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a comprehensive tour through varied landscapes.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling tours starting from Noáin (Valle De Elorz)?
A: The region offers several long-distance routes for those looking for extended rides. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39546167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Egozkue – Rabia Bridge, Zubiri loop from Cocheras Noain&lt;/a&gt; covers over 114 km, providing a substantial challenge and a full day of cycling through diverse terrain.
tips: Beautiful and quiet town at the foot of the great Higa and an obligatory step on the Camino de Santiago. Its little streets give the feeling of traveling back in time and in part it is, this medieval town has been known since the 12th century, although it is suspected that it may be even older.

This small hamlet of barely 50 inhabitants (2008) seems to have been lost in time.
It is the capital of the municipality of Ibargoiti and therefore the town hall is located here, one of the most outstanding buildings next to the Church of San Clemente.

Flanked by the Aragón River and a town on the Camino de Santiago pass for centuries, it is said that this town has a monument on every street and you can see for yourself. Among the sites, the convent of San Francisco de Asís and del Carmen and the palace of Ongay-Vallesantoro stand out among other historical sites from Roman times.
Beautiful views of the picturesque town of Eslava on the panoramic road that joins Estella and Sangüesa passing through the Alto de Lerga.
